methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Neustark is a Series B climate & energy company founded by Valentin Gutknecht and Johannes Tiefenthaler that has raised $70M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies across the same sector and stage—1 public (LNZA) and 8 private firms including KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, Amogy, Helion, and Redwood Materials.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $70M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $133M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: KoBold Metals at $3.0B (Series C), Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B (Series C), and Amogy at $700M (Series C). The median across these three peers is $1.0B, with a range of $700M to $3.0B.

The triangulated analysis yields a valuation range of $113M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $566M. The wide spread reflects material stage discount between Neustark's Series B position and the Series C valuations of sampled peers, offset by sector tailwinds evident in peer multiples. Key uncertainties include technology validation maturity relative to peers, customer offtake commitments, and the timing of revenue inflection in the climate-tech sector.
